title: Anil ultima
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Anil ultima". However, I can suggest an alternative plant that matches this name, Anil or Anisomeles. 

Anil or Anisomeles (Anisomeles malabarica) is a plant species that belongs to the Lamiaceae family, commonly known as the mint or deadnettle family. It is a shrubby or woody climber native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, including India, China, and Southeast Asia.

Anil ultima, or Anisomeles, is believed to have various health benefits, including its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy in treating various health conditions.

The bioactive constituents of Anil ultima include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents may include compounds such as flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids.

Anil ultima is commonly used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts. It is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
